county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sheridan County formed 1885 from Sioux County. County seat is Rushville. Northwestern Panhandle. Borders Pine Ridge Reservation (South Dakota). Mari Sandoz country. Ranching.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sheridan County (formed 1885, seat Rushville) in the northwestern Panhandle was carved from Sioux County. Records are at the courthouse in Rushville. Sheridan County borders the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation in South Dakota. Researchers with Lakota/Sioux connections should check NARA Kansas City for Pine Ridge Agency records. Settlement came through homesteading and ranching. Hay Springs, Gordon, and Rushville were the main communities. BLM GLO records document land entries. Anglo-American and German settlers predominated. FamilySearch has limited microfilm. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. The Mari Sandoz Heritage Society in Gordon preserves the legacy of the noted Nebraska author who documented Sandhills ranch life.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Sheridan County. County Register of Deeds in Rushville holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1885. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Kinkaid Act Homestead Records -- Sheridan County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kinkaid Act (1904) homestead records for Sheridan County. The Kinkaid Act allowed 640-acre homestead claims in the Sandhills and other western Nebraska counties, recognizing that 160 acres was insufficient for ranching in semi-arid grasslands. Original case files at NARA; patents at BLM GLO.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sherman County formed 1871 from unorganized territory. County seat is Loup City. Central Nebraska. Middle and South Loup rivers. Significant Polish settlement in Loup City area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sherman County (formed 1871, seat Loup City) in central Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in Loup City. The Middle Loup and South Loup rivers run through the county. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. Polish settlers were significant in the Loup City area -- Polish Catholic parish records are important. German and Anglo-American settlers also contributed. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Sherman County. County Register of Deeds in Loup City holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sioux County formed 1877 from unorganized territory. County seat is Harrison. Nebraska's most northwestern county. Pine Ridge area. Agate Fossil Beds National Monument. Ranching.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sioux County (formed 1877, seat Harrison) is Nebraska's most northwestern county in the Pine Ridge area of the Panhandle. Records are at the courthouse in Harrison. Agate Fossil Beds National Monument is in the county. Sioux County was one of the last areas to be settled in Nebraska. The county borders Pine Ridge and Oglala Sioux reservations in South Dakota. Ranching dominates the economy. BLM GLO records document homestead entries. Anglo-American settlers predominated. FamilySearch has limited microfilm. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Sioux County. County Register of Deeds in Harrison holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Stanton County formed 1862 from Dodge and Madison counties. County seat is Stanton. Northeast Nebraska. German heritage. Elkhorn River.
county_metadata.research_tips: Stanton County (formed 1862, seat Stanton) records are at the courthouse in Stanton. Stanton County is in northeastern Nebraska and attracted German settlers -- German Lutheran and Catholic church records are important. The Elkhorn River runs through the county. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Stanton and Pilger were the main communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Stanton County. County Register of Deeds in Stanton holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Thayer County formed 1871 from Jefferson County. County seat is Hebron. South-central Nebraska. Little Blue River valley. German, Czech/Bohemian settlement. Burlington Railroad.
county_metadata.research_tips: Thayer County (formed 1871, seat Hebron) in south-central Nebraska was created from Jefferson County. Records are at the courthouse in Hebron. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. The Little Blue River valley influenced settlement. German, Czech/Bohemian, and Anglo-American settlers predominated. Burlington and Missouri River Railroad brought settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Deshler's German-American community is notable. Hebron newspapers from the 1870s are valuable sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Thayer County. County Register of Deeds in Hebron holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Thomas County formed 1887 from unorganized territory. County seat is Thedford. Sandhills county. Nebraska National Forest at Halsey. Cattle ranching. Kinkaid Act homesteads.
county_metadata.research_tips: Thomas County (formed 1887, seat Thedford) is a Sandhills county with one of Nebraska's smallest populations. Records are at the courthouse in Thedford. The Kinkaid Act (1904) was essential for permanent settlement. BLM GLO records document land entries. Cattle ranching is virtually the only industry. The Nebraska National Forest at Halsey (the largest hand-planted forest in the Western Hemisphere) is partly in Thomas County. FamilySearch has minimal coverage. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Researchers should check neighboring Blaine and Hooker county records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Thomas County. County Register of Deeds in Thedford holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Thurston County formed 1889 (as Blackbird, renamed). County seat is Pender. Mostly comprises Omaha and Winnebago Indian Reservations. Dr. Susan La Flesche Picotte, first Native American woman physician.
county_metadata.research_tips: Thurston County (formed 1889 as Blackbird County, renamed 1889, seat Pender) is unique because most of its area comprises the Omaha and Winnebago Indian Reservations. Records are at the courthouse in Pender. For Omaha tribal records, check NARA Kansas City for Omaha Agency records -- tribal census rolls (1885-1940), allotment records, annuity rolls, school records (Omaha Mission School), and BIA correspondence. For Winnebago (Ho-Chunk) records, check NARA Kansas City for Winnebago Agency records -- the Winnebago were relocated to Nebraska from Minnesota in 1863. Walthill is the center of the Omaha Reservation; Winnebago is the center of the Winnebago Reservation. Non-Native settlement is in the eastern part of the county. FamilySearch has some microfilmed records. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Dr. Susan La Flesche Picotte, first Native American woman physician, was from the Omaha Reservation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Thurston County. County Register of Deeds in Pender holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Omaha and Winnebago Agency Records -- Thurston County
source_profiles.repository: NARA Kansas City / Bureau of Indian Affairs
source_profiles.coverage_notes: BIA agency records for the Omaha and Winnebago (Ho-Chunk) reservations. Tribal census rolls (1885-1940), allotment records, annuity rolls, school records, correspondence, and vital statistics. The Winnebago were relocated from Minnesota in 1863. Access may require tribal approval for some records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BIA records at NARA are substantially complete; tribal records may have access restrictions

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Valley County formed 1871 from unorganized territory. County seat is Ord. Central Nebraska. North Loup River valley. Danish settlement. Seventh-Day Adventist community.
county_metadata.research_tips: Valley County (formed 1871, seat Ord) in central Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in Ord. The North Loup River valley runs through the county. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. Danish settlers were significant in the Ord-North Loup area. Danish Lutheran church records are important. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Ord newspapers from the 1880s are useful sources.

source_profiles.source_name: Valley County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Valley County including homestead entries, pre-emption claims, timber culture entries, cash sales, and military warrant locations. Nebraska is a public land state -- all original land transfers from federal ownership are documented here. Searchable online by name and location.

source_profiles.source_name: Valley County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / Historical Society
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Valley County Historical Society / Nebraska State Genealogical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions and burial records for Valley County. Find A Grave has growing coverage. Township, church, and family cemeteries are common in rural Nebraska. County historical societies and the Nebraska State Genealogical Society have published cemetery transcription volumes.

source_profiles.source_name: Valley County Records on Microfilm -- History Nebraska
source_profiles.coverage_notes: History Nebraska (NSHS) holds microfilm copies of Valley County records including deed indexes, probate records, marriage records, county board proceedings, and selected court records. Also holds manuscript collections, photographs, and maps related to Valley County history.

source_profiles.source_name: Valley County Danish Lutheran Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Danish American Archive / Grand View University / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Danish Lutheran church records for Valley County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Dana College (Blair) archives may hold related records. FamilySearch has microfilmed some collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Washington County formed 1854 as original Nebraska Territory county. County seat is Blair. Missouri River county. Dana College (Danish Lutheran). Fort Atkinson (1820-1827). Strong Danish heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Washington County (formed 1854, seat Blair) is one of the original Nebraska Territory counties along the Missouri River. Records are at the courthouse in Blair. Dana College (Danish Lutheran, 1884-2010) was in Blair -- its archives may be at History Nebraska or the Danish Immigrant Museum in Elk Horn, Iowa. Washington County has strong Danish heritage. Being an original county, Washington has territorial-era records from 1854. Fort Atkinson (1820-1827) was the first US military post west of the Missouri River -- pre-territorial military records at NARA. Homestead and pre-emption entries at BLM GLO document settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Washington County. County Register of Deeds in Blair holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Washington County Register of Deeds, Blair
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1854 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1854. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Probate and Estate Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1854. Nebraska probate is handled by the County Court. Records include wills, letters of administration, estate inventories, guardianship files, and adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census records for Washington County (1860-1950). The 1890 census was destroyed by fire. Nebraska conducted a state census in 1885 (available on microfilm). Territorial censuses (1854-1856, 1860) exist for early counties. Available through FamilySearch and Ancestry.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1890 federal census destroyed nationally; Nebraska 1885 state census survives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wayne County formed 1871 from unorganized territory. County seat is Wayne. Northeast Nebraska. Wayne State College (1891). Strong German heritage. Elkhorn River valley.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wayne County (formed 1871, seat Wayne) in northeast Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in Wayne. Wayne State College (1891) is located here. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. German settlers were particularly numerous. German Lutheran and Catholic church records are important. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Wayne newspapers from the 1880s provide genealogical information.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Wayne County. County Register of Deeds in Wayne holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.repository: Wayne County Register of Deeds, Wayne
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1871 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Wayne County German Lutheran and Catholic Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Omaha / Diocese of Lincoln / Diocese of Grand Island / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Lutheran and Catholic church records for Wayne County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Records often in German. German-Russian (Volga German) congregations may have separate records.
